channel.title: Zephyr Project — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by Zephyr Project (zephyr).

item.title: CVE-2026-10642 · 6.5 MEDIUM — Unbounded TX busy-loop DoS in Zephyr PL011 UART driver under CTS hardware flow control
item.description: The Zephyr PL011 UART driver (drivers/serial/uart_pl011.c) contains an unbounded software loop in pl011_irq_tx_enable() that repeatedly invokes the interrupt-driven application callback while the TX interrupt mask bit (PL011_IMSC_TXIM) is set, to wor…

item.title: CVE-2026-10658 · 7.1 HIGH — Bluetooth Host ISO RX Missing SDU Header Length Validation in bt_iso_recv() Leads to DoS
item.description: A missing length validation in the Zephyr Bluetooth Host ISO receive path can be triggered by malformed HCI ISO data. In bt_iso_recv() (subsys/bluetooth/host/iso.c), when processing PB=START/SINGLE fragments, the code pulls a TS SDU header (8 bytes,…

item.title: CVE-2026-10651 · 7.1 HIGH — Bluetooth Classic SDP parser truncation bug in bt_sdp_parse_attribute() leads to reachable assertion and possible out-of-bounds read
item.description: A malformed Bluetooth Classic SDP attribute can trigger a reachable assertion in Zephyr's SDP parser. In subsys/bluetooth/host/classic/sdp.c, bt_sdp_parse_attribute() accepts an input buffer once it contains the 1-byte attribute type and 2-byte attri…

item.title: CVE-2026-10645 · 4.9 MEDIUM — fs: ext2: Missing structural validation of directory entries can cause out-of-bounds read and zero-progress directory traversal
item.description: Zephyr's ext2 directory-entry parser does not fully validate on-disk directory entry structure before copying the entry name and advancing traversal state. In ext2_fetch_direntry() (subsys/fs/ext2/ext2_diskops.c), the code only checks de_name_len <=…

item.title: CVE-2026-10641 · 7.1 HIGH — Out-of-bounds write in Bluetooth HFP Hands-Free CIND indicator parsing (cind_handle_values)
item.description: Zephyr's Bluetooth Classic Hands-Free Profile (HFP) Hands-Free role parser (subsys/bluetooth/host/classic/hfp_hf.c) contains an out-of-bounds write. During Service Level Connection setup the HF sends AT+CIND=? and parses the AG's +CIND: response in c…

item.title: CVE-2026-10640 · 4.2 MEDIUM — Use-after-free reading `net_pkt` `iface` after send in IPv6 Neighbor Discovery (`ipv6_nbr.c`)
item.description: Zephyr's IPv6 Neighbor Discovery send paths (net_ipv6_send_na, net_ipv6_send_ns, net_ipv6_send_rs in subsys/net/ip/ipv6_nbr.c) updated the per-interface ICMP-sent statistics by calling net_pkt_iface(pkt) after net_send_data(pkt) had already returned…

item.title: CVE-2026-10639 · 4.8 MEDIUM — Use-after-free reading `net_pkt_iface()` of a sent ICMPv4 echo-reply packet in `icmpv4_handle_echo_request()`
item.description: In Zephyr's native IPv4 stack, icmpv4_handle_echo_request() in subsys/net/ip/icmpv4.c builds an echo-reply packet (reply), hands it to net_try_send_data(), and then, on success, calls net_stats_update_icmp_sent(net_pkt_iface(reply)). net_try_send_dat…

item.title: CVE-2026-10638 · 5.9 MEDIUM — Use-after-free in Zephyr ICMPv6 RX path when updating statistics after sending an echo reply or error
item.description: subsys/net/ip/icmpv6.c reads the network interface from a net_pkt after that packet has been handed to net_try_send_data(). In icmpv6_handle_echo_request() and net_icmpv6_send_error(), the post-send statistics update calls net_pkt_iface(reply)/net_pk…

item.title: CVE-2026-10637 · 5.9 MEDIUM — Use-after-free of net_pkt in IPv6 MLD send path triggerable by a link-local MLD Query
item.description: subsys/net/ip/ipv6_mld.c:mld_send() read the packet interface via net_pkt_iface(pkt) after net_send_data(pkt) returned successfully. Per the network stack's ownership contract (include/zephyr/net/net_core.h, and the explicit warning in subsys/net/ip/…

item.title: CVE-2026-10636 · 3.7 LOW — Use-after-free in Zephyr IPv4 IGMP send path (igmp_send)
item.description: In Zephyr's IPv4 IGMP implementation, igmp_send() in subsys/net/ip/igmp.c read the network interface back out of the packet via net_pkt_iface(pkt) after the packet had been handed to net_send_data(). On the successful-send path the packet's last refe…

item.title: CVE-2026-10635 · 6.3 MEDIUM — Dangling memory-domain pointer (use-after-free) in Xtensa MMU page-table code on memory-domain de-init
item.description: On Xtensa targets with CONFIG_USERSPACE and CONFIG_XTENSA_MMU, the page-table code (arch/xtensa/core/ptables.c) maintains a global list, xtensa_domain_list, of active memory domains using a list node embedded inside the caller-owned struct k_mem_doma…

item.title: CVE-2026-10634 · 4.8 MEDIUM — Use-after-free in Zephyr native TCP net_tcp_foreach() due to dropping tcp_lock during the callback
item.description: Zephyr's native TCP stack iterates the global connection list in net_tcp_foreach() (subsys/net/ip/tcp.c) using the SYS_SLIST_FOR_EACH_CONTAINER_SAFE macro, which caches a pointer to the next list node. Prior to this fix the function released tcp_lock…

item.title: CVE-2026-5068 · 7.6 HIGH — bt: l2cap le coc: remote oob write via seg counter stored in net_buf user_data
item.description: A remote, unauthenticated BLE peer can trigger a 2-byte out-of-bounds write in the Bluetooth host during L2CAP LE CoC SDU reassembly. When the application enables segmentation (via chan_ops.alloc_buf) and the chosen RX pool has a user_data_size small…

item.title: CVE-2026-5066 · 6.3 MEDIUM — net: sockets: tls: Potential out-of-bounds write/read in socket_op_vtable::connect function
item.description: A potential out-of-bounds write/read exists in the TLS socket connect path of the network sockets subsystem (subsys/net/lib/sockets/sockets_tls.c). When the TLS session cache is enabled, tls_session_store() and tls_session_restore() memcpy the caller…

item.title: CVE-2026-5589 · 6.3 MEDIUM — Out-of-bounds write caused by an integer underflow in the Bluetooth Mesh subsystem.
item.description: An integer underflow in bt_mesh_sol_recv() in the Bluetooth Mesh solicitation handling (subsys/bluetooth/mesh/solicitation.c) leads to an out-of-bounds write. When CONFIG_BT_MESH_OD_PRIV_PROXY_SRV is enabled, the function parses solicitation PDUs fro…

item.title: CVE-2026-5071 · 6.1 MEDIUM — can: Local Denial of Service via SocketCAN Send
item.description: The SocketCAN implementation validates the length of a user-provided buffer containing a socketcan_frame object using only a NET_ASSERT statement in zcan_sendto_ctx() before dereferencing it in socketcan_to_can_frame(). In production builds where ass…

item.title: CVE-2026-5072 · 6.5 MEDIUM — ptp: Potential Denial of Service via PTP Interval Shift
item.description: A bitwise shift vulnerability in Zephyr's PTP subsystem allows a remote attacker to cause undefined behavior and potential system crashes. An attacker sends a crafted PTP_MSG_MANAGEMENT message to set an unvalidated negative log_announce_interval val…

item.title: CVE-2026-1681 · 6.1 MEDIUM — net: Stack Overflow with Ping (to own IP Address) via Shell
item.description: Issuing an ICMP ping via the `net ping` shell command to a device's own IPv4 address causes the network stack to recursively re-enter the input path on the same system work-queue stack. Because the destination is recognized as a local address, both t…

item.title: CVE-2026-1677 · 5.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — net: TLS 1.2 connections allowed on TLS 1.3 sockets
item.description: Zephyr sockets created with `IPPROTO_TLS_1_3` can still negotiate a TLS 1.2 connection when both TLS versions are enabled in Kconfig, because the socket-level protocol selection is not propagated to mbedTLS (e.g. via `mbedtls_ssl_conf_min_tls_version…

item.title: CVE-2026-5590 · 6.4 MEDIUM — net: ip/tcp: Null pointer dereference can be triggered by a race condition
item.description: A race condition during TCP connection teardown can cause tcp_recv() to operate on a connection that has already been released. If tcp_conn_search() returns NULL while processing a SYN packet, a NULL pointer derived from stale context data is passed…

item.title: CVE-2026-1679 · 7.3 HIGH — net: eswifi socket send payload length not bounded
item.description: The eswifi socket offload driver copies user-provided payloads into a fixed buffer without checking available space; oversized sends overflow `eswifi->buf`, corrupting kernel memory (CWE-120). Exploit requires local code that can call the socket send…

item.title: CVE-2026-4179 · 6.1 MEDIUM — stm32: usb: Infinite while loop in Interrupt Handler
item.description: Issues in stm32 USB device driver (drivers/usb/device/usb_dc_stm32.c) can lead to an infinite while loop.

item.title: CVE-2026-0849 · 3.8 LOW — crypto: ATAES132A response length allows stack buffer overflow
item.description: Malformed ATAES132A responses with an oversized length field overflow a 52-byte stack buffer in the Zephyr crypto driver, allowing a compromised device or bus attacker to corrupt kernel memory and potentially hijack execution.

item.title: CVE-2026-1678 · 9.4 CRITICAL 🌩️ — dns: memory‑safety issue in the DNS name parser
item.description: dns_unpack_name() caches the buffer tailroom once and reuses it while appending DNS labels. As the buffer grows, the cached size becomes incorrect, and the final null terminator can be written past the buffer. With assertions disabled (default), a ma…

item.title: CVE-2025-12899 · 6.5 MEDIUM 🌩️ — net: icmp: Out of bound memory read
item.description: A flaw in Zephyr’s network stack allows an IPv4 packet containing ICMP type 128 to be misclassified as an ICMPv6 Echo Request. This results in an out-of-bounds memory read and creates a potential information-leak vulnerability in the networking subsy…

item.title: CVE-2025-12035 · 6.5 MEDIUM — Bluetooth: Integer Overflow in Bluetooth Classic (BR/EDR) L2CAP
item.description: An integer overflow condition exists in Bluetooth Host stack, within the bt_br_acl_recv routine a critical path for processing inbound BR/EDR L2CAP traffic.

item.title: CVE-2025-9557 · 7.6 HIGH — Bluetooth: Mesh: Out-of-Bound Write in gen_prov_cont
item.description: ‭An out-of-bound write can lead to an arbitrary code execution. Even on devices with some form of memory protection, this can still lead to‬ ‭a crash and a resultant denial of service.‬

item.title: CVE-2025-9558 · 7.6 HIGH — Bluetooth: Mesh: Out-of-Bound Write in gen_prov_start
item.description: There is a potential OOB Write vulnerability in the gen_prov_start function in pb_adv.c. The full length of the received data is copied into the link.rx.buf receiver buffer without any validation on the data size.

item.title: CVE-2025-9408 · 8.2 HIGH — Userspace privilege escalation vulnerability on Cortex M
item.description: System call entry on Cortex M (and possibly R and A, but I think not) has a race which allows very practical privilege escalation for malicious userspace processes.

item.title: CVE-2025-12890 · 6.5 MEDIUM — Bluetooth: peripheral: Invalid handling of malformed connection request
item.description: Improper handling of malformed Connection Request with the interval set to be 1 (which supposed to be illegal) and the chM 0x7CFFFFFFFF triggers a crash. The peripheral will not be connectable after it.

item.title: CVE-2025-10456 · 7.1 HIGH — Bluetooth: Semi-Arbitrary ability to make the BLE Target send disconnection requests
item.description: A vulnerability was identified in the handling of Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) fixed channels (such as SMP or ATT). Specifically, an attacker could exploit a flaw that causes the BLE target (i.e., the device under attack) to attempt to disconnect a fix…

item.title: CVE-2025-10458 · 7.6 HIGH — Bluetooth: le_conn_rsp does not sanitize CID, MTU, MPS values
item.description: Parameters are not validated or sanitized, and are later used in various internal operations.

item.title: CVE-2025-7403 · 7.6 HIGH — Bluetooth: bt_conn_tx_processor unsafe handling
item.description: Unsafe handling in bt_conn_tx_processor causes a use-after-free, resulting in a write-before-zero. The written 4 bytes are attacker-controlled, enabling precise memory corruption.

item.title: CVE-2025-10457 · 4.3 MEDIUM — Bluetooth: Out-Of-Context le_conn_rsp Handling
item.description: The function responsible for handling BLE connection responses does not verify whether a response is expected—that is, whether the device has initiated a connection request. Instead, it relies solely on identifier matching.

item.title: CVE-2025-2962 · 8.2 HIGH 🌩️ — Infinite loop in dns_copy_qname
item.description: A denial-of-service issue in the dns implemenation could cause an infinite loop.

item.title: CVE-2025-1675 · 8.2 HIGH 🌩️ — Out of bounds read in dns_copy_qname
item.description: The function dns_copy_qname in dns_pack.c performs performs a memcpy operation with an untrusted field and does not check if the source buffer is large enough to contain the copied data.

item.title: CVE-2025-1674 · 8.2 HIGH 🌩️ — Out of bounds read when unpacking DNS answers
item.description: A lack of input validation allows for out of bounds reads caused by malicious or malformed packets.

item.title: CVE-2025-1673 · 8.2 HIGH 🌩️ — Out of bounds read when calling crc16_ansi and strlen in dns_validate_msg
item.description: A malicious or malformed DNS packet without a payload can cause an out-of-bounds read, resulting in a crash (denial of service) or an incorrect computation.

item.title: CVE-2024-10395 · 8.6 HIGH 🌩️ — net: lib: http_server: Buffer Under-read
item.description: No proper validation of the length of user input in http_server_get_content_type_from_extension.

item.title: CVE-2024-8798 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Bluetooth: classic: avdtp: missing buffer length check
item.description: No proper validation of the length of user input in olcp_ind_handler in zephyr/subsys/bluetooth/services/ots/ots_client.c.

item.title: CVE-2024-11263 · 9.4 CRITICAL — arch: riscv: userspace: potential security risk when CONFIG_RISCV_GP=y
item.description: When the Global Pointer (GP) relative addressing is enabled (CONFIG_RISCV_GP=y), the gp reg points at 0x800 bytes past the start of the .sdata section which is then used by the linker to relax accesses to global symbols.

item.title: CVE-2024-6444 · 6.3 MEDIUM — Bluetooth: ots: missing buffer length check
item.description: No proper validation of the length of user input in olcp_ind_handler in zephyr/subsys/bluetooth/services/ots/ots_client.c.

item.title: CVE-2024-6443 · 6.3 MEDIUM — zephyr: out-of-bound read in utf8_trunc
item.description: In utf8_trunc in zephyr/lib/utils/utf8.c, last_byte_p can point to one byte before the string pointer if the string is empty.

item.title: CVE-2024-6442 · 6.3 MEDIUM — Bluetooth: ASCS Unchecked tailroom of the response buffer
item.description: In ascs_cp_rsp_add in /subsys/bluetooth/audio/ascs.c, an unchecked tailroom could lead to a global buffer overflow.

item.title: CVE-2024-6259 · 7.6 HIGH — BT: HCI: adv_ext_report Improper discarding in adv_ext_report
item.description: BT: HCI: adv_ext_report Improper discarding in adv_ext_report

item.title: CVE-2024-6137 · 7.6 HIGH — BT: Classic: SDP OOB access in get_att_search_list
item.description: BT: Classic: SDP OOB access in get_att_search_list

item.title: CVE-2024-6135 · 7.6 HIGH — BT:Classic: Multiple missing buf length checks
item.description: BT:Classic: Multiple missing buf length checks

item.title: CVE-2024-5931 · 6.3 MEDIUM — BT: Unchecked user input in bap_broadcast_assistant
item.description: BT: Unchecked user input in bap_broadcast_assistant

item.title: CVE-2024-6258 · 6.8 MEDIUM — BT: Missing length checks of net_buf in rfcomm_handle_data
item.description: BT: Missing length checks of net_buf in rfcomm_handle_data

item.title: CVE-2024-5754 · 8.2 HIGH — BT: Encryption procedure host vulnerability
item.description: BT: Encryption procedure host vulnerability
